records.ety: [AS. ēkan, kan; akin to OFries. āka, OS. ōkian, OHG. ouhhōn to add, Icel. auka to increase, Sw. öka, Dan. öge, Goth. aukan, L. augere, Skr. ōjas strength, ugra mighty, and probably to English wax, v. i. Cf. Augment, Nickname.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Eked (ēkt); p. pr. & vb. n. Eking.]

defs: To increase; to add to; to augment; -- now commonly used with out, the notion conveyed being to add to, or piece out by a laborious, inferior, or scanty addition; as, to eke out a scanty supply of one kind with some other.
quotes: He eked out by his wits an income of barely fifty pounds.

records.ety: [AS. ec; akin to OFries. k, OS. <?/k, D. <?/ok, OHG. ouh, G. auch, Icel. auk, Sw. och and, Dan. og, Goth. auk for, but. Prob. from the preceding verb.]

quotes: 'T will be prodigious hard to prove That this is eke the throne of love.
A trainband captain eke was he Of famous London town.
notes: ☞ Eke serves less to unite than to render prominent a subjoined more important sentence or notion.

quotes: Clumsy ekes that may well be spared.
